title: redaction_result_{document_id}.json -- PII redaction record, one file per document v0.2
description: Record of what was redacted from one document's OCR'd text before it entered the processed layer. Produced by document-pipeline's checkpoint 2 -- one file per document (redaction_result_{document_id}.json), since checkpoint 2 runs once per document and a single shared file across documents would get silently overwritten by each subsequent document's write (write-contract replaces the whole file; see known-gaps.md's audit of this exact failure mode, first found in ocr_result.json). v0.1 nested everything under a documents: [] array to cover multiple documents in one file -- v0.2 flattens that away since each file now covers exactly one document. Mechanism is a general LLM for now (see open-decisions.md #1 -- OpenMed, a self-hosted PHI/PII NER suite, is a flagged candidate replacement, not yet adopted).

items_redacted.description: Count of PII items redacted from this document's text (names, resident registration numbers, etc.).

categories.description: Kinds of PII found. e.g. person_name, resident_registration_number, phone_number, address

filename_pii_flagged.description: True if the ORIGINAL filename (before intake renamed it to a document_id-based name) appeared to contain PII. Content redaction alone doesn't fix a leaking filename -- intake's rename to DOC_XXX.<ext> is what actually prevents this; this field is a diagnostic flag, not a fix applied here.
